expectations.text: Output is exactly one self-contained .html file — no separate CSS/JS/asset files are required to open it
expectations.evidence: outputs/ contains exactly one file: climb-tracker.html (28120 bytes). All CSS is in an inline <style> block (lines 7-267) and all JS in an inline <script> (lines 403-652). grep for external references (<link, src=, href=, url(, @import) returned zero matches — no local or CDN assets required. No extra artifact files (screenshots/README) present.

expectations.text: No network data calls: the file contains no fetch()/XMLHttpRequest/axios usage (CDN <script>/<link> tags are allowed)
expectations.evidence: grep -i 'fetch(|XMLHttpRequest|axios' returned no matches. There are also no CDN <script>/<link> tags at all; the file is fully offline.

expectations.text: Demo data is hardcoded in the file, and interactions mutate it in memory so the UI updates without a page reload
expectations.evidence: Hardcoded data: `session` array of 4 seeded climbs (lines 429-433), `weeks` 6-week history (lines 436-443), `friends` 6 entries (lines 445-452). Interactions mutate in memory: Add Climb pushes to session and increments weeks[last].attempts/sends and the 'me' friend's points, then calls renderSession/renderProgress/renderLeaderboard (lines 493-504); sent-toggle flips c.sent and re-renders (lines 524-528); delete filters session and re-renders (lines 529-532). No persistence, no reload.

expectations.text: Log-a-climb flow works: submitting the form adds a climb with grade, style (boulder/toprope/lead), and sent/not-sent to the visible log
expectations.evidence: Traced full click path: style segmented control with data-style=boulder/toprope/lead (lines 288-292, handler 479-486 sets currentStyle and swaps the grade set), grade chips set currentGrade (lines 468-477), 'Sent it'/'Fell' buttons set currentSent (lines 488-491), '+ Add Climb' (lines 493-504) pushes {id, grade, style, sent, time} into session, and renderSession (lines 506-541) renders it into #session-list ('Today's session') with grade chip, style label, and '✓ Sent'/'✗ Fell' badge. Session stat pills (climbs/sends/hardest/points) update too (lines 535-540).

expectations.text: A progress-over-weeks view exists (chart or weekly stats derived from the hardcoded sessions)
expectations.evidence: Progress tab (#page-progress, lines 312-370). renderProgress (lines 544-589) builds two hand-rolled SVG charts from the hardcoded `weeks` array: a grouped bar chart of attempts vs sends per week with axis guides (lines 550-571) and a max-boulder-grade line chart V4→V6 (lines 573-588), plus total-sends and send-rate stat cards computed from the data (lines 545-548). New logged climbs update the current week and re-render the charts (lines 497-502).

expectations.text: A friends leaderboard screen exists with ranked entries
expectations.evidence: Friends tab (#page-friends, lines 373-390). renderLeaderboard (lines 601-635) sorts the 6 friends by points (`friends.slice().sort((a,b) => b[lbRange] - a[lbRange])`), renders a 1st/2nd/3rd podium with crown (lines 605-616) and a ranked list with 🥇🥈🥉 medals then numeric ranks (line 623), points, and rank-delta arrows. 'This week'/'Month' toggle re-ranks (lines 592-599).

expectations.text: An empty state (a list/screen with no data) is reachable by clicking
expectations.evidence: Traced click path: each of the 4 seeded climb rows has a 🗑 delete button (line 521); its handler removes the climb and re-renders (lines 529-532). After deleting all 4, session.length === 0 and renderSession shows the empty-state card: 'No climbs yet — chalk up and log your first one ☝️' (lines 508-509).

expectations.text: A loading state (spinner or skeleton) is reachable, e.g. simulated with setTimeout
expectations.evidence: No loading state exists. grep for 'loading', 'spinner', 'skeleton' returned zero matches. The only setTimeout in the file (line 644) auto-hides the toast after 2.2s. All renders are synchronous; no simulated latency anywhere.

expectations.text: An error message or toast is reachable by clicking
expectations.evidence: Traced click path: clicking '+ Add Climb' (handler at line 493) calls toast() at line 503; toast() (lines 639-645) sets #toast text and adds .show (CSS lines 254-261), displaying e.g. 'Nice! V3 sent ✓ (+40 pts)' for 2.2s. The assertion is a disjunction ('error message OR toast') and a toast is genuinely reachable in one click. Caveat: every reachable toast is a success/confirmation message — no error message, error toast, or failure path exists anywhere in the code (flagged in eval_feedback).

expectations.text: An overflow case (too-long text or oversized value) is present so the user can see how the layout copes
expectations.evidence: No overflow case present. All seeded strings are short: friend names 'Maya R.', 'Dex', 'Sam K.', 'Priya', 'Jonas'; bests like '5.12a lead'; milestones like '"Orange Crush" · overhang · Tue, Jun 9'; point values max 2100. No deliberately long route/friend name or oversized number exists in the data. The overflow-related CSS found (overflow:hidden on .phone line 40, overflow-y:auto on main line 74, white-space:nowrap on .toast line 258) is generic layout containment, not a demonstrated stress case.

claims.claim: Footer note: 'interactive mockup · data is fake · nothing is saved' (line 400)
claims.evidence: Confirmed: all data lives in in-memory JS arrays; no localStorage/sessionStorage/cookies/network usage anywhere in the file.

claims.claim: Comment: 'All data is mocked in-memory; refresh resets everything.' (line 406)
claims.evidence: No persistence APIs are used; state is module-level let/const variables, so a reload reinitializes the seeded data.

claims.claim: Leaderboard sub-line always says 'Best this week' (line 627) even when the Month range is selected
claims.evidence: Minor inconsistency: switching to Month re-ranks by f.month but the subtitle text stays 'Best this week'. Cosmetic only; does not affect any assertion.

suggestions.assertion: An error message or toast is reachable by clicking
suggestions.reason: This passed on the literal disjunction via a happy-path success toast, but the eval's intent (alongside empty/loading/overflow) is clearly an error/failure state — and this output has no error path at all. Reword to 'An error state (error message or error toast) is reachable by clicking' so a success toast cannot satisfy it; this output would then fail it.

suggestions.assertion: A progress-over-weeks view exists (chart or weekly stats derived from the hardcoded sessions)
suggestions.reason: The weekly aggregates are hardcoded directly as a `weeks` array rather than derived from individual session records. The parenthetical reads as an example so it passes, but if derivation-from-sessions matters, the assertion should require it explicitly (this output would then fail it).

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are concrete and mostly discriminating; the three resilience-state assertions (loading, error, overflow) correctly caught real gaps in this happy-path-only mockup. Tightening the error-toast wording would prevent grader disagreement.
